exercises.prompt: Implement doubleAll(arr) so it returns a new array in which every number from arr has been multiplied by 2. An empty array returns an empty array.
hints: arr.map(fn) builds a brand-new array by running fn on each element — exactly the 'transform every item' shape.
The transform is a doubling: x => x * 2.
map already handles the empty array — mapping over [] returns [] — so you need no special case.

exercises.prompt: Implement sumEven(arr) so it returns the sum of only the even numbers in arr. An empty array, or one with no even numbers, returns 0.
hints: Declare an accumulator at 0 before the loop, then add to it as you find even numbers.
x % 2 === 0 tests evenness, and it holds for negatives and zero too (-4 and 0 are both even).
Return the accumulator after the loop — if nothing was even it is still 0, which is also the empty-array answer.

exercises.prompt: Implement countVowels(str) so it returns how many vowels (a, e, i, o, u) the string contains, counting both uppercase and lowercase. An empty string returns 0.
hints: Lowercase the whole string first with str.toLowerCase() so 'A' and 'a' count the same.
Walk the characters with a for...of loop and test each one against the vowels.
'aeiou'.includes(ch) is a compact membership test — increment a counter each time it is true.
